elements.title: deep-water soloing
elements.contents: ... that several climbers have drowned while deep-water soloing (example pictured), even though it is safer than free solo climbing?

elements.title: John Allen Chau
elements.contents: ... that John Allen Chau illegally travelled to North Sentinel Island in an attempt to convert its inhabitants to Christianity?

elements.title: Martin-Smith School of Music
elements.contents: ... that the Martin-Smith School of Music, according to one historian, was "one of the most important black musical institutions" in the United States?

elements.title: Luca Gargano
elements.contents: ... that Luca Gargano discovered thousands of barrels of aged rum in an abandoned Trinidadian distillery, sparking "Caroni mania"?

elements.title: a 1980 Doctor Who serial
elements.contents: ... that a 1980 Doctor Who serial was written by a seventeen-year-old fan of the show?

elements.title: Annie Stainer
elements.contents: ... that Annie Stainer "would appear to fly, by delicately and continuously undulating her arms as if they were remarkable wings" during her solo shows?

elements.title: Tomodachi Collection
elements.contents: ... that Shigeru Miyamoto told his coworkers "you guys are useless" after he received a prototype of Tomodachi Collection's character-creation system?

elements.title: Eric Slover
elements.contents: ... that Eric Slover's wife blessed his dog tags with holy water before Operation Absolute Resolve?

elements.title: A Place with the Pigs
elements.contents: ... that A Place with the Pigs was based on a real-life World War II deserter who hid in a pigsty until 1985?
